rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Of The Fifth, Sixth And Seventh Causes Of Action In The First Amended Cross-Complaint

CROSS COMPLAINANT 888 SEVENTH STREET OWNERS ASSOCIATION MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Of The Fifth, Sixth And Seventh Causes Of Action In The First Amended Cross-Complaint is DENIED. rulings.Case Title: 1ST STOCKTON & CLAY, LLC VS. STOCKTON SEAFOOD CENTER, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION / Notice Of Motion & Motion For Orders Terminating Stipulated Stay And Entering Written Summary Judgment Order And Entry Of Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 29, 2024 line 7. PLAINTIFF 1ST STOCKTON & CLAY, LLC MOTION / Notice Of Motion & Motion For Orders Terminating Stipulated Stay And Entering Written Summary Judgment Order And Entry Of Judgment is DENIED without prejudice. It appears from the moving papers that after the Court ruled on a Motion for Summary Judgment, the parties entered into a side agreement to stay the proceedings. Moving papers do not address exactly what authority the Court is to employ to "undo" the parties' stipulated agreement as there is no discussion how this private agreement is "a court process" or a "court order" that the Court may control per CCP 128. There is no record of the Court having ever been apprised of this agreement or that the Court was requested to formally stay the action. It does appear from the Court records that the Court received a proposed order on the Motion for Summary Judgment and the order was rejected with instructions to comply with CRC 3.1312 and CCP 437c(g) before re-submitting the order for execution. There is no record that an amended compliant proposed order has ever been received. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: 1ST STOCKTON & CLAY, LLC VS. STOCKTON SEAFOOD CENTER, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Application For Leave To Intervene, Memorandum Of Points And Authorities, Declaration, Proof Of Service
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 29, 2024 line 7. DEFENDANT IN INTERVENTION XUE FENG Application For Leave To Intervene, Memorandum Of Points And Authorities, Declaration, Proof Of Service is DENIED. No authority is cited for the proposition that the Complaint-in-Intervention seeking declaratory and injunctive relief is available in unlawful detainer actions. Additionally, per the Court record, the Court has already granted a Motion for Summary Judgment in this action and, other than a ministerial act of entering the order and the judgment, there is nothing more to be addressed on the merits. The Court does note that the parties appear to have reached a side agreement unbeknownst to the Court that might prevent the parties from asking the Court to execute those documents at this time. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: SAAM BARRAGER ET AL VS. LAURA DOUGLASS DECARAVA
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 29, 2024 line 8. DEFENDANT LAURA DECARAVA MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is GRANTED. Defendant shifter their burden and no triable issue of fact exists regarding Plaintiff's failure to serve Form 1007 in effect at the time of the service of the operative Notice to vacate. Plaintiff cites no authorities for the proposition that any form 1007 ever prepared by the Board satisfies the requirement of the SFRO to server "the form." =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849).
